question_frequency.rationale: The Pair (acting as the assistant) asked essentially no didactic questions. Turn 1 just dumps the optimal solution; Turn 2 only asks 'A or B?' as a cosmetic pick after already labeling A as 'Best'; Turn 3 just summarizes. No teachable forks were genuinely posed to the user.

question_quality.rationale: The single choice presented (hash map vs two-pointer) was pre-decided — Option A is explicitly tagged 'Best' and B 'Requires index mapping' before the user picks. No real crux question (e.g., why store after checking, why complement lookup beats nested loop) was actually put to the user as a decision.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no progression — the full optimal solution with complexity analysis and edge cases is revealed in Turn 1 before any user reasoning is invited. The 'choice' in Turn 2 is a spoiler since the answer is labeled in the option headers.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the prose is clean, but the conversation is a monologue rather than back-and-forth. The Pair preempts the user's thinking, then congratulates them; there's no genuine dialogue or actionable prompts for the learner.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
